question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for his assessment of whether his Department has sufficient resources to ensure that all movements of animals under licence fully complies with the conditions of that licence.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for her assessment of why the Comptroller and Auditor General issued a disclaimed opinion in relation to her Department's 2023-24 annual report and accounts.
answer_text: The Comptroller and Auditor General (C&AG) has disclaimed her audit opinion on the basis of Prior Period Adjustments that the Department for the Economy (DfE) made to the 31 March 2023 comparative figures in the 31 March 2024 Accounts.  While this is disappointing, the adjustments were identified by DfE and arose as a result of the Department implementing a new, more robust model to produce the Group Accounts for the year ended 31 March 2024. The Disclaimer Opinion on Student Loans relates to the C&AG’s view on the model used to estimate Student Loan assets.  The model used to estimate the value of these assets was created by the Department for Education in England (DfE England).  However, in 2021-22, DfE England moved away from this model to a new model which it considers provides a better basis for the valuation.  Whilst the model used by DfE continues to be maintained by DfE England, there is a risk that it becomes more out of date and increasingly unreliable in areas such as the use of up-to-date information on earnings projections.  It is the intention of DfE to move to the new model for the 2025-26 financial year which will provide more accurate information in relation to the Student Loan asset in DfE’s Accounts.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail all road resurfacing works completed in Lisbellaw village in the last five years, including the associated cost per year.
answer_text: Details of the resurfacing work carried out in Lisbellaw is provided in the Department’s Annual Reports to Fermanagh & Omagh Council. I have provided the links to the relevant reports for your convenience. https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/publications/annual-report-fermanagh-and-omagh-district-council-2020https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/publications/annual-and-interim-reports-fermanagh-and-omagh-district-council-2021https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/publications/annual-report-fermanagh-and-omagh-district-council-2022https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/publications/annual-report-fermanagh-and-omagh-district-council-2023https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/articles/fermanagh-and-omagh-council-report-2024My Department’s spend on resurfacing schemes in Lisbellaw village in the last 5 years totals £135,000.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether he will assess if the Housing Executive will be able to accept occupational therapy assessments from the Victims and Survivors Service in future.
answer_text: Occupational Therapists (OTs) are nominated on behalf of Health & Social Care Trusts to carry out assessments to determine the needs of disabled people, including their housing needs.  If an adaptation is required the OT will make a recommendation to the Housing Executive and, where appropriate, will inspect the adaptation when it is completed to ensure it is adequate for the client’s needs. This is a statutory responsibility of the Health & Social Care Trusts.Private OTs cannot fulfil this statutory obligation as legislation requires external and professional opinion by the relevant Health authority on whether the works are necessary and appropriate to meet the needs of the disabled occupant.Consequently, private OT reports from any source cannot be accepted to take forward adaptation works in any property, be it Housing Executive stock or adaptations for a private sector property funded by a Disabled Facilities Grant.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to outline how the budget will deliver the Programme for Government 2024-2027.
To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any steps his Department is taking to address the shortage of affordable social housing in the Enniskillen area.
answer_text: Housing need is generally addressed through the re-let of existing social housing accommodation (Stock turnover). Where the volume of re-lets is insufficient to meet housing need, then construction or acquisition of further (new) social housing is required.In the Enniskillen Area:There has been 1 social housing unit completed over the last five years 2019/20 - 2024/25.  There are currently 40 social housing units under construction.  There are 170 social housing units programmed to start as part of the Social Housing Development Programme (SHDP) 2024/25 – 2027/28.  Please note that programmed schemes can be lost or slip to future programme years for a variety of reasons e.g. relating to delays in acquiring sites and/or failure to secure Planning Approval. Additional schemes can also be added to the SHDP ‘in-year’ through Competitive Land, Design & Build procurement route and/or the purchase of Existing Satisfactory / Off-the-Shelf properties.Wider than this, the Executive Housing Supply Strategy published on 9th December identifies the need for a whole system approach to address housing supply issues. The Strategy recognises there is not one single overarching challenge impacting on supply. As we move to implementation of the Strategy, addressing these challenges will depend on Executive commitment to invest in the actions required to deliver the supply we need.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail all road resurfacing works completed in Moygashel village in the last five years, including the associated cost per year.
answer_text: My Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government and this has had a significant impact on its ability to deliver essential services such as road maintenance. There are many roads that would undoubtedly benefit from investment, including those in Moygashel Village, however, due to a limited budget, resurfacing work must be taken forward through the prioritisation of those deemed most in need of intervention.Details of the resurfacing work carried out in Moygashel is provided in the Department’s Annual Reports to Mid Ulster Council. For your convenience I have provided the relevant links to the documents https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/publications/annual-report-mid-ulster-district-council-2020https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/publications/annual-and-interim-reports-mid-ulster-district-council-2021https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/publications/annual-report-mid-ulster-district-council-2022https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/articles/mid-ulster-district-council-report-2023https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/articles/mid-ulster-district-council-report-2024My Department’s spend on resurfacing schemes in Moygashel village in the last 5 years totals £70,000.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any work that has been carried out on whether a dispensation will apply to Northern Ireland in the event of United States of America tariffs on the United Kingdom or European Union, when a differential occurs between the jurisdictions.
answer_text: The US is a significant market for the economy in the North. I continue to closely monitor developments and actively advocate for the interests of the North's economy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 22121/22-27, to detail (i) the points of contact her Department has had with any UK Departments on the matters of common interest relating to planning; (ii) the scope of any meetings and discussions held with UK Departments on this matter; (iii) any meetings held with UK Departments; and (iv) any planned meetings with UK Departments going forward.
answer_text: My Department’s Planning Group is in regular contact with various Ministries / Departments in the other UK jurisdictions (as well as the South) on a variety of matters of mutual interest. The meetings and discussions cover a range of areas including local development and spatial planning, renewables, forthcoming legislation, cases and investigations by other European and international bodies such as the Aarhus Convention Compliance Committee, and the regular ‘5 Administration’ meetings between the Chief Planners of the England, Scotland, Wales, North and South Ireland. My Department will continue to meet and actively engage with colleagues in the other jurisdictions on matters affecting the North.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether they have received an invitation to the White House for St. Patrick's Day celebrations.
answer_text: Following a request from the State Department for nominations for attendance at the White House reception, invitations were issued to those who had elected to be nominated, which included the deputy First Minister. The First Minster declined the nomination and therefore the offer of an invitation.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether she has identified any steps that could be taken by her Department or Executive colleagues to address the high cost of car insurance.
answer_text: I acknowledge that the cost of motor insurance is a concern for many on our roads. My Department has no direct legislative influence on the prices set by insurance companies, which are regulated by the Financial Conduct Authority.I do, however, recognise that my Department can assist by improving road safety and, in turn, lower the number of collisions on our roads, which is a major contributory factor to insurance costs.The industry body, the Association of British Insurers published a ten-step roadmap in 2024, aimed at tackling insurance costs for all drivers.From those ten steps, four fall within the scope of my Department.These steps are: 1) Work with partners to minimise uninsured driving, contributing to fewer collisions; 2) improving road safety & infrastructure;3) supporting new and novice drivers; and4) advocating for safety-focused vehicle technology.Alongside this work to assist with the reduction of motor insurance costs, I recognise the importance of considering the potential effects on insurance premiums of policy decisions taken by my Department. Therefore, in making future policy decisions – including any proposals to manage MoT testing arrangements – I will consider the potential impact on insurance premiums.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to outline any steps his Department is taking to address the shortage of affordable social housing in the Dungannon area.
answer_text: Housing need is generally addressed through the re-let of existing social housing accommodation (Stock turnover). Where the volume of re-lets is insufficient to meet housing need, then construction or acquisition of further (new) social housing is required.In the last 5 years from 2019/20 until 2024/25 a total of 143 new social homes were completed within the Dungannon area. 29 of these have been completed so far this year. A further 111 social homes are currently under construction.  There are 46 social housing units programmed to start as part of the Social Housing Development Programme (SHDP) 2024/25 – 2027/28 in the Dungannon area.  Wider than this, I published an Executive Housing Supply Strategy focussed on addressing the whole system issues that impact on overall supply across all of Northern Ireland. This means the Executive has made a commitment to developing actions needed to address supply across all tenures.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any consideration he has given to a new school build for Fivemiletown Primary School.
answer_text: Fivemiletown Primary School does not feature on the Major Capital Works Programme of announced projects. Due to the number of projects which have been announced under the Major Capital Works Programme but have not yet been delivered, I do not intend to make a new call for projects in the near future.   As a controlled school any nomination under a future call for projects will need to be made by the Education Authority.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the financial cap on Land and Property Services valuations in relation to compulsory purchases to facilitate the A5 Western Transport Corridor project, following vesting orders in November 2024.
answer_text: ANSWERThere is no financial cap on valuations undertaken by Land & Property Services (LPS). Compensation for the vested land / property on the A5 will be based on market values at 25th November 2024.The Department for Infrastructure administers additional statutory payments which do have a financial cap. These are the Basic and Owner Occupier Loss payments plus the Home Loss payments.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the current Land and Property Services market value for agricultural land in County Tyrone, in the context of purchases to facilitate the A5 Western Transport Corridor project following vesting orders in November 2024.
answer_text: ANSWERLand & Property Services (LPS) has been in discussion with agents representing claimants to discuss market sales evidence. Negotiations of claims for advance payments submitted to the Department for Infrastructure (DfI) by appointed agents have commenced. It is the role of the agent to substantiate and negotiate the claims with LPS, and each claim is considered on its own merits. There are variable factors which will determine land values on the A5 route such as location, quality of the land and topography.My officials will not provide details of market value discussions this early in the scheme, to avoid prejudicing ongoing compensation negotiations.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the total amount of compensation paid to landowners on compulsory purchases to facilitate the A5 Western Transport Corridor project, following vesting orders in November 2024.
answer_text: ANSWERTo date no compensation has been paid following the statutory vesting of the A5 Western Corridor which became operative on 25th November 2024. 		A number of claims for advance payment have been submitted to the Department for Infrastructure (DfI) and are under consideration by Land and Property Services (LPS). Payment of compensation will be a matter for DfI.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether she will explore a part-time 20mph speed limit outside Fivemiletown Primary School.
answer_text: A review of the initiative introducing part-time 20mph speed limits at schools has been undertaken and is available on my Department’s internet site Review of Part-Time 20mph Speed Limits at Schools | Department for Infrastructure.The review recommends that the Department should provide a third tranche of part-time 20 mph speed limits at schools, however this will be more modest in size and is dependent on the availability of funding. The report also concludes that a more targeted approach is needed to ensure that the correct safety intervention is selected and work updating our policy document is underway.   Funding for progressing the next tranche of part-time 20 mph speed limits at schools will be included within budget planning considerations for next financial year.I would also like to see what can be done to improve attitudes to speeding, not only for road safety purposes, but to make places cleaner and greener.  As part of my consideration of the recently published review I have asked my officials to prepare a paper on 20mph speed limits in general taking into consideration schools, residential areas and places where there is a higher number of those walking, wheeling and cycling.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether she intends to update Lough Erne (Navigation) Byelaws (Northern Ireland) 1978.
answer_text: Waterways Ireland intends to update the Lough Erne (Navigation) (Amendment) Bye-Laws (Northern Ireland) 1986 and the Lough Erne (Navigation) Bye-Laws (Northern Ireland) 1978.It is planned the work to revise the Erne Bye Laws will commence before the end of the year; however this is dependent on the revised Bye Laws for the Shannon and Canals being approved.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail any support being given to film studios in Northern Ireland through the rates system, in light of the HM Treasury announcement of 40 per cent relief on business rates for the sector until 2034.
answer_text: ANSWERPremises used as film studios here can be eligible for Industrial Derating if their primary use is the manufacture and design of film sets, and props and costumes for the film productions carried out on site. Industrial Derating amounts to a 70% relief on the normal annual rates liability, is unique to here and sits at a level 30% higher than the specific subsidy for film studios announced in England

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether she intends to update Lough Erne (Navigation) (Amendment) Bye-laws (Northern Ireland) 1986.
answer_text: Waterways Ireland intends to update the Lough Erne (Navigation) (Amendment) Bye-Laws (Northern Ireland) 1986 and the Lough Erne (Navigation) Bye-Laws (Northern Ireland) 1978.It is planned the work to revise the Erne Bye Laws will commence before the end of the year; however this is dependent on the revised Bye Laws for the Shannon and Canals being approved.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any consideration her Department has given to zoning parts of Lough Erne for recreational use for water craft.
answer_text: Waterways Ireland, as the navigation authority, is responsible for enforcement of the Lough Erne (Navigation) Bye-Laws (Northern Ireland) 1978 and the Lough Erne (Navigation) (Amendment) Bye-Laws (Northern Ireland) 1986. These bye-laws include vessel speed limits (zoning) which seek to manage the speed of differing types of recreational watercraft.Waterways Ireland intends to update these bye-laws which will involve a public consultation process during which input will be invited from stakeholders.  It is planned the work to revise the Erne Bye Laws will commence before the end of the year; however, this is dependent on the revised Bye Laws for the Shannon and Canals being approved.However, the recently completed phase of the regeneration of the Ulster Canal in Clones by Waterways Ireland has recreational water facilities, therefore, this is a possibility.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice when she will outline her response to the recommendations of the review of the Northern Ireland Policing Board.
To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline the impact of high grid connection charges on the local economy.
answer_text: Question 9 AQO 1593/22-27The underlying unit cost of making new connections, and the charges for them, are similar here compared to the South and Britain, what is different is the level of socialisation of reinforcement costs to the distribution network.  The Utility Regulator reviews and benchmarks the unit costs chargeable by the network companies for connections, to ensure they are efficient and in the interests of electricity customers.The cost of reinforcement to the existing distribution network can increase connection charges for some.  The proportion of this cost which is socialised here, is currently lower than in the South or Britain, so those customers currently pay more.  My Department is consulting on increasing the socialisation of these reinforcement costs.  This will help reduce the costs for individuals seeking to connect, especially in rural areas where the grid is not as strong.  The consultation will close on 24 April and implementation of any changes is targeted for the end of the year. Final changes will be the responsibility of the Utility Regulator and NIE Networks.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to the UK Government's Planning Reform Working Paper: Development and Nature Recovery, whether her Department has given any consideration to integrating development with nature recovery, to encourage better planning outcomes and better environmental protection in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: While DAERA and NIEA would have overall responsibility for nature conservation in the North, my Department works collaboratively with them on a number of environmental matters, including supporting sustainability, enhancing biodiversity and contributing to nature recovery, as part of the planning process.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) any consideration her Department has given to the UK Government’s Planning Reform Working Paper: Development and Nature Recovery, in order to help inform better planning outcomes; and (ii) any discussions her Department has had with the (a) Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government; and (b) Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, in order to foster collaboration and knowledge sharing to achieve better outcomes for planning.
answer_text: I and my officials are aware of the UK Government’s working paper on Development and Nature Recovery. As planning is a devolved matter, no legislative requirements from these proposals will be introduced in the North. I can confirm that my Department has established points of contact with UK Departments on a number of matters of common interest relating to planning.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any assistance she is providing to telecommunication broadband companies in obtaining permits to carry out repairs on public roads.
answer_text: My officials work closely with telecommunication broadband companies to process Traffic Control Permits to facilitate repairs on the public road.There is a well established procedure for obtaining permits generally requiring a 3-week period from submission of the permit until the date of the works. However, this requirement can be relaxed, especially if the permit relates to short term emergency works.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for an update on any steps he has taken to financially support the Fermanagh Classic and Pike Classic fishing competitions on Lough Erne in 2025.
answer_text: Budget pressures have required prioritisation of resources to deliver statutory obligations and the management of the Public Angling Estate. However, my department will continue to support the Fermanagh Classic and the Pike Classic by making the Public Angling Estate available for use.Officials have had constructive engagement with Fermanagh and Omagh District Council to enable support for the Fermanagh Classic. The department will subsequently engage with the organisers of the Erne Pike Classic to agree arrangements.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether his Department has began assessing the damage caused to the Forest Service estate following Storm Éowyn, broken down by forests affected in terms of acres.
answer_text: Forest Service has commenced an analysis of the extent of woodlands damaged across the forest estate by Storm Éowyn.  Following initial assessments, significant impacts have been identified in forests across many areas including Drum Manor, Gosford, Tardree, Tollymore, Castlewellan, Riversdale, Lough Navar, Favour Royal, Fardross, Mullaghafad and Kesh. However, the area affected, in these and other forests, has still to be quantified.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, further to the closure of Lack Primary School, whether he has any plans to increase enrolment and admission numbers for Kesh Primary School for the 2025-2026 academic year.
answer_text: The decision to close Lack Primary School from August 2025 was taken on 6 January.  Since that time, Education Authority staff have been supporting parents of children currently in attendance at the school and helping advise on alternative school settings.There is sufficient capacity at alternative Controlled schools in the area to cater for those children who will be leaving Lack PS.  Indeed, at the last census date for which figures are available, there were a total of 141 places available in Controlled schools which are situated closer to Lack Primary School than Kesh Primary School.In respect of Kesh Primary School, there should be sufficient capacity within the school to accommodate all children leaving Lack Primary School, without any requirement for an increase in the school’s approved numbers, if that is the wish of parents.If the school does fill to capacity, the school may apply to my Department for a Temporary Variation to facilitate the admission of additional children.Should the school wish to seek a permanent increase to its numbers, as a Controlled school, it would be the responsibility of the EA, as Managing Authority, to bring forward a Development Proposal.  Therefore, if the Board of Governors wishes to increase the school’s approved admissions and enrolment numbers, it should liaise with the EA as its Managing Authority in the first instance.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail (i) the number of coarse angling permits active in Lough Erne since 19 March 2021; and (ii) the number of applications for a coarse angling permit in Lough Erne since 2021, broken down per fishing season.

A coarse angling permit allows the holder to fish through-out the Public Angling Estate coarse fisheries, which includes Lough Erne. question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail (i) the number of game angling permits active in Lough Erne since 19 March 2021; and (ii) the number of applications for a game angling permit in Lough Erne since 2021, broken down per fishing season.
answer_text: There is no specific permit for game angling on Lough Erne. A Public Angling Estate (PAE) game permit allows the holder to fish through-out the PAE, which includes Lough Erne. question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs (i) for an update on the situation at Movanagher fish farm following the discovery of invasive zebra mussels; and (ii) when his Department expects it to be operational again.
answer_text: The presence of Zebra Mussels is still preventing the stocking of fish from Movanagher Fish Farm. I will be considering options for future stocking of the Public Angling Estate, including the 2025 season, in the coming weeks.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail any studies his Department has carried out to measure the economic, social and cultural benefits that angling brings to (i) Northern Ireland; and (ii) Co. Fermanagh.
answer_text: The Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs (DAERA) has not carried out studies, however relevant studies prior to DAERA’s formation include:Northern Ireland: The benefits of angling have been previously identified in the Strategic Review of Angling in Northern Ireland, undertaken in 2014 by Sport NI, the NI Tourist Board, the Loughs Agency and the Department of Culture Arts and Leisure.  Co. Fermanagh: As laid out in the DAERA Lough Erne Fisheries Management Plan from 2022, a study was carried out by Price Waterhouse Cooper in 2007 on the social and economic impact on Northern Ireland (and areas within the Loughs Agency) of recreational fisheries, angling and angling resources.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs when the review into the Public Angling Estate will be completed.
answer_text: I anticipate being able to announce the outcome of review into the Public Angling Estate by autumn 2025.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether his Department has sought alternative fish farms for stocking the Public Angling Estate whilst stockings from Movanagher fish farm have been suspended.
answer_text: No decisions have been made regarding the stocking of the Public Angling Estate in 2025.Although stocking from Movanagher Fish Farm has been suspended, the farm still operates and incurs running costs to maintain fish stocks until a stocking protocol is established to allow for the future stocking of fish in compliance with the Wildlife Order.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail any punitive measures in place for those who ignore suspended movement of animals from Great Britain to Northern Ireland and are discovered to have smuggled livestock, endangering the health status of Northern Ireland's herds.
answer_text: All those who breach Departmental statutory requirements including those who ignore the suspension of animal movements from GB-NI will be handled in accordance with my Department’s enforcement policy which is published on the Departmental website.

answer_text: I noted the outcome of the British Government’s dialogue with China and the commitments made in the areas of financial services, trade and climate. The detail of the agreement is a matter for the British Government.I continue to engage with British Ministers and others to ensure our economic interests are furthered on the international stage.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the number of incidents of livestock smuggling from Great Britain reported since the bluetongue restrictions in GB in 2024.
answer_text: My Department have intercepted one incident of livestock smuggling from Great Britain (GB) since the direct movement of all live ruminants and camelids from GB to Northern Ireland (NI) was suspended in November 2023 due to the detection of Bluetongue in GB. I would like to emphasize the critical importance of adhering to the importation requirements that are in place as these are vital to protect NI from devastating disease outbreaks.By disregarding importation requirements individuals risk compromising NI’s animal health status, with significant impacts for animal welfare, NI farmers and the wider agricultural sector.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

A cost estimate for decant works at the former College site has been provided by the Integrated Consultant Team as part of the technical design and pre-tender estimate (RIBA Stage 4), which is currently being considered by the Department’s technical advisers in Education Advisory Branch.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the number of MOT tests undertaken at each MOT test centre during the 2022-2027 mandate, broken down by (i) passes; (ii) fails; and (iii) retests.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the options that are available to him in the event of a bluetongue outbreak.
answer_text: Bluetongue is listed as a Category C disease under EU Regulation 2016/429, the Animal Health Law.  Category C diseases are deemed of relevance to some EU Member States (including Northern Ireland) and for which measures are needed to prevent it from spreading to parts of the EU and Northern Ireland that are officially disease-free or that have eradication programmes for the listed disease concerned. Under the Animal Health Law it is optional to establish an eradication programme for Category C diseases. In the event of a Bluetongue outbreak in Northern Ireland options available to my Department regarding a disease control response will include serving restriction notices on suspected infected premises, conducting veterinary investigations, sampling of susceptible species and implementation of disease control zones.  In addition, cleansing and disinfection of affected premises would be carried out and where appropriate vector control methods could be deployed. Furthermore, based on the epidemiological picture and where proportionate culling of infected animals would also be considered.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities what steps he is taking to support self-employed individuals, including farmers, impacted by the minimum income floor for Universal Credit.
To ask the Minister of Justice to outline any plans to upgrade Enniskillen Courthouse.

To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he will commit to retaining all Department for Infrastructure jobs based at Castle Barracks, Enniskillen, when the office is disposed of by the Department of Finance in April 2025.
answer_text: The Department of Finance is taking forward an Office Estate Strategy to deliver a right-sized, more modern, greener, and fit for purpose office environment.The Estate Strategy has been ongoing from 2022 and all DoF accommodation is constantly under review to ensure the Civil Service reduces its carbon footprint and maximises its office accommodation by collaborating across sites.A decision has not been made to close DfI Offices at Castle Barracks. The property at Castle Barracks has been identified as needing substantial investment with a condition survey from September 2022 recommending replacement of the heating system and pipework in the building (at an estimated minimum cost of circa £250k). The site holds circa 42 workstations and therefore, before investing a significant amount of money, Properties Division is working with officials from my Department to consider whether DfI’s accommodation needs could be met using under-utilised space at other DoF properties in Enniskillen.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance, pursuant to the disposal of the Northern Ireland Civil Service office at Castle Barracks, Enniskillen, in April 2025, to detail any engagement her Department has had with other stakeholders in the Castle Barracks complex in relation to this disposal.
answer_text: A decision has not been made to close or dispose of the office at Castle Barracks, Enniskillen. Discussions between officials in my Department’s Properties Division and Department for Infrastructure (DfI) colleagues to assess DfI’s office accommodation needs in Enniskillen are at an early stage.The Department’s property at Castle Barracks is occupied solely by DfI and has been identified as needing substantial investment, with a condition survey from September 2022 recommending replacement of the heating system and pipework. Once the feasibility of options has been explored, a business case will be developed by Properties Division in cooperation with DfI representatives, to determine whether best value for money would be achieved by retaining the property or relocating staff to a different location in Enniskillen.I have been advised that Trade Union Side has been notified that accommodation options in Enniskillen are being considered and local consultation will be taken forward through the normal channels.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether a final decision has been made on the relocation of the Enniskillen DFI Roads Service Office at Castle Barracks.
answer_text: The Department of Finance (DoF) are responsible for the NICS Estate. The property at Castle Barracks has been identified as needing substantial investment with a condition survey from September 2022 recommending replacement of the heating system and pipework in the building.  DoF Properties Division are working with my officials to determine if my Department’s accommodation needs could be met using under-utilised space at other DoF properties in Enniskillen.  No final decision has been made on the potential relocation of staff at the Castle Barracks site.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he will commit to assessing the need for traffic calming measures on the Middle Road, Saintfield.
answer_text: Road safety remains a high priority for my Department and we are committed to working proactively to make our roads safer and address the needs of all road users.Middle Road, Saintfield currently benefits from a number of safety measures to raise driver awareness such as: enhanced, grey-backed 30mph signs with supplementary red textured surfacing; a 30mph roundel road marking approaching Saintfield; and streetlighting along the through route.My Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government and receives many requests for road safety improvements, including traffic calming.Due to this continued underfunding, only those requests requiring most urgent attention can be prioritised and to do so, each site is assessed against the current departmental policy.  A recent assessment indicates that Middle Road has a good safety record and as such no further measures are being considered at this time.Concerns regarding driver behaviour such as speeding should be directed to the PSNI using their non-emergency 101 number.One of the best ways to ensure safety on our roads and fulfil our long-term goal of eliminating death and serious injury by 2050, is by changing road user behaviour. As road users we all have a personal responsibility to behave in a way that keeps ourselves and others safe.  The sad reality is that if we do not change our own attitudes when we are using the roads, our death toll will continue to increase.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail a timeline for the completion of the resurfacing of Main Street, Saintfield.
answer_text: In September 2014, Lower Main Street, Saintfield was resurfaced as a major scheme which lowered the road surface and implemented a full reconstruction of the road pavement.My Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government. This has had a significant impact on our ability to deliver essential services such as road maintenance. While many roads including Upper Main Street, Saintfield would undoubtedly benefit from investment, due to a limited budget, resurfacing work must be taken forward through the prioritisation of those deemed most in need of intervention. In the meantime, officials in my Department will continue to undertake regular inspections and repair defects in accordance with the Department’s policy.

I have asked my officials to consider the report carefully, and to provide me with advice on the cost and legislative implications of each of the recommendations made. question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for his assessment on the impact of changes to Agricultural Property Relief will have on Northern Ireland farm businesses.
answer_text: An assessment undertaken by DAERA suggests that the limitation of agricultural property relief and business property relief at a rate of 100% to a combined value of £1m will affect around one third of all Northern Ireland farms in terms of the value of land owned by the farm exceeding £1m in agricultural value.  This increases to around 75% of dairy farms. However, it is important to note these figures are estimates as they cannot take into account land ownership structure within farms, development value and are based on an average agricultural market value. Further detail of this analysis is available at the NI Assembly Library (https://www.niassembly.gov.uk/assembly-business/research-and-information-service-raise/deposited-papers-2024/).  I recognise that these changes in inheritance tax rate relief will have significant ramifications for the farming community and would encourage farmers to seek advice from professional tax advisors regarding the impacts to their farms. I will do all that I can, as Minister to support the farming community in Northern Ireland on this issue although it is not a devolved matter.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to outline the total cost associated with resurfacing work on the Sydenham bypass, with comparison to the resurfacing work undertaken on the A4 from Enniskillen to Ballygawley during the 2022-27 mandate to date.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) the number of job vacancies that exist in each of the DFI Roads divisions; and (ii) the steps he is taking to address the shortfall in staff in these divisions.
answer_text: My Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government. This has had a significant impact on my Department’s ability to deliver essential services, and to fill vacant posts. The number of Full Time Equivalent (FTE) vacancies in each of the DfI Roads Divisions is shown in the table below, along with the % vacancy rate:DfI Roads DivisionNumber of Vacancies (FTE)% Vacancy RateNorthern 34.424%Southern42.025%Eastern29.5617%Western35.222%NICS HR, within the Department of Finance, has overall responsibility for NICS recruitment, and DfI supports this to ensure that, as far as is possible, the right people with the right skills are in the right posts within the Department. My Department has taken a number of steps to address the high vacancy rate including working with NICS HR to progress a range of recruitment competitions; the establishment of a People Development & Workforce Planning Directorate and agreement of a Workforce Plan to focus efforts on recruitment to fill critical vacancies. In addition, the Department has taken forward recruitment campaigns to provide career pathways into DfI through Graduate, Apprenticeships and the pilot DfI Professional & Technical Skills Academy which, has been successful in identifying suitable candidates to take up positions in key areas such as road and private streets inspectors.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to outline (i) what measures she is taking to tackle rural crime in border regions, and (ii) what resources she is allocating to address rural crime.
answer_text: A partnership approach is central to addressing crime, including agricultural crime, in all regions. The Department of Justice chairs the Rural Crime Partnership (RCP) with members including the Police Service of Northern Ireland (PSNI), the Ulster Farmers’ Union, NFU Mutual, the Young Farmers’ Club of Ulster, the Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs (DAERA) and Policing and Community Safety Partnerships (PCSPs). The partnership shares information on emerging or reoccurring crimes, and will continue to do so, to support a coordinated response and preventative approach to addressing agricultural crime. RCP members, led by the PSNI, also launched the first NI specific Rural Crime Week on 23 September 2024.  This social media campaign was aimed entirely at raising awareness of rural crime issues and encouraging rural communities to report such crimes. 	At a local level, PCSPs, which are funded jointly by my Department and the Northern Ireland Policing Board, play a key role in addressing crime, including agricultural crime, and building confidence locally, through engagement and consultation with communities, including rural areas. PCSPs play a pivotal role in supporting communities in identifying solutions to issues of local concern.  They also deliver a range of initiatives to raise awareness and support rural communities and businesses to protect their property and goods.  These initiatives include trailer/equipment marking events and such actions have helped to continue the downward trend in recorded rural and agricultural crime.Notwithstanding the above, the operational detection and investigation of agricultural crime, including how resources are deployed, is an operational matter for the Chief Constable, who is accountable to the Northern Ireland Policing Board.  I am committed to respecting the operational independence of the Chief Constable and the role of the NI Policing Board, and you may therefore wish to also direct your question to the PSNI.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for an update on the current status of bovine TB in the Fermanagh and South Tyrone constituency.
answer_text: Information relating to bovine Tuberculosis (bTB) is not held on a constituency basis, rather, it is held on the basis of the geographical regions covered by Divisional Veterinary Office (DVO) boundaries.  The Fermanagh and South Tyrone Constituency that you have requested information on, mostly falls within the Enniskillen DVO boundary, however some of it falls within the Dungannon DVO, and a very small amount of it within the Armagh DVO.The herd and animal incidence figures (published in August 2024) for the Enniskillen, Dungannon and Armagh DVOs, as well as the Northern Ireland average figures are recorded in the tables below.Table 1 – Annual herd incidence figures.August 2024 statisticsNorthern IrelandArmaghDungannonEnniskillenAnnual herd incidence over the last 12 months (%)10.159.1811.011.23Annual herd incidence over the last 13-24 months (%)10.719.949.3414.98Table 2 – Annual animal incidence figures.August 2024 statisticsNorthern IrelandArmaghDungannonEnniskillenAnnual animal incidence over the last 12 months (%)1.1430.9811.0381.781Annual animal incidence over the last 13-24 months (%)1.0020.8780.6921.957DAERA publishes statistics on the bovine Tuberculosis programme on a monthly basis on the DAERA website and these can be found using the following link: https://www.daera-ni.gov.uk/articles/tuberculosis-statistics-northern-ireland
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any discussions he has had with NI Water in relation to the dumping of raw sewage in Lough Erne.
answer_text: The management of public wastewater infrastructure is an operational matter for NI Water. However, I have sought the requested information from NI Water and have been advised as follows:NI Water does not dump raw sewage into Lough Erne, rather after wastewater has been treated at wastewater treatment works, the final treated effluent is discharged into Lough Erne through outfall pipes. During periods of heavy rainfall, diluted wastewater may also be discharged into Lough Erne from storm overflows.    It is important to note that all sewer systems and wastewater pumping stations are designed with emergency spill points. These spill points are required to reduce the risk of sewage escaping from sewers during periods of heavy rainfall and causing flooding to homes, schools and businesses, which would represent a significant public health risk. Spills are made up of wastewater from households, industry and commerce that is heavily diluted by the rainwater and an estimated 80-95% of what is spilled each year comprises rainwater. The remainder is what causes most harm to the environment; foul water that is made up of everyday wastewater from kitchens and bathrooms in homes, as well as wastewater from businesses and public buildings. NI Water estimates that around 1-2% of what is spilled is raw sewage.I met regularly with the Chair of the NI Water Board and both the Non-Executive and Executive Board Members as required.   For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact NI Water directly, through the Elected Representatives Hotline on 0345 300 6461 or through waterline@niwater.comMaking direct contact with NI Water will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the number of water pollution incidents in Upper and Lower Lough Erne that have been reported to the NIEA in the last two years.
answer_text: Reports of water pollution received by my Department, in respect of Upper and Lower Lough Erne, and those reports, that, following investigation, led to confirmed incidents of water pollution in the last 2 years are as follows: Water Pollution IncidentsLower Lough ErneUpper Lough Erne20222023Total20222023TotalReported40641046979148Confirmed 283967405595Each report was investigated in person by officials of the Northern Ireland Environment Agency.Where an incident is confirmed, its Environmental Impact is classified as High, Medium or Low Severity. The Environmental Impact of the incidents confirmed in 2022 and 2023 in Upper and Lower Lough Erne are as follows:Confirmed IncidentsLower Lough ErneUpper Lough Erne20222023Total20222023TotalLow263157375289Medium Severity2810336Total283967405595There were no incidents, during that time period, where the Environmental Impact was assessed as High Severity.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy how his Department will ensure source material, required for the production of biomethane, will be priced fairly and competitively to ensure it is a cost-effective energy source for end users.
answer_text: My Department is currently working through a call for evidence which includes a particular focus on the economics of developing local biomethane production.  We will continue to liaise closely with DAERA on issues concerning the effective management of feedstocks, and with the Utility Regulator, as we draw up a policy framework for biomethane which includes consideration of fair and competitive pricing for consumers at its core.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the funding his Department has allocated for the Energy Strategy, broken down by policy framework.
answer_text: I have allocated £124m for the Energy Strategy, broken down as follows: Northern Ireland Sustainable Energy Programme (NISEP) - £3.2m; Energy & Resource Efficiency (ERE) Programme for Businesses - £40m;Invest to Save Fund - £73m;  Green Innovation Challenge Fund - £4.5m; and, GeoEnergy NI Project (geothermal energy) - £3m.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail (i) the procedure and protocol for reporting odour pollution to the NIEA; and (ii) the methodology the NIEA uses to determine odour pollution.
answer_text: Depending on the suspected source of the odour pollution, reports can be made to 028 9056 9747 or via email to NIEAwastecomplaint@daera-ni.gov.uk (for waste sites) or 028 9056 9289 or email ipri@daera-ni.gov.uk (for other industrial sites). Complainants are requested to provide the following information:•	The time and date the odour was detected; •	Whether the odour was intermittent or constant;•	The location where the odour was detected; and•	A description of the odour detected.The NIEA uses the guidance available at the following link to determine odour pollution: https://www.daera-ni.gov.uk/publications/odour-impact-assessment-guidance-permitted-and-licensed-sites
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the work his Department (i) has undertaken; and (ii) plans to take to promote net-zero technology with large-scale manufacturing businesses.
answer_text: Invest NI provides Energy and Resource Efficiency support to help businesses implement green efficiencies, achieve cost savings and carbon savings, and progress towards Net-Zero.This support includes Technical Consultancy, a Resource Matching Service, the Resource Efficiency Capital Grant, and sustainability reports. Invest NI has also recently launched the Energy Efficiency Capital Grant which  provides grant support to buy and install energy efficient equipment, which includes net zero technologies, that reduce energy and carbon emissions.Invest NI is also leading on the Industrial Decarbonisation for Northern Ireland (IDNI) project.   Over 220 of the North’s largest energy users have been actively engaged in the project, with workshops delivered across all council areas to ensure broad participation. Funded by Innovate UK, IDNI is a prime example of leveraging alternative funding to meet our decarbonisation goals.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the recent call for evidence on developing biomethane production in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: My Department launched its Call for Evidence (CfE) on biomethane on 17th May 2024 and, in collaboration with DAERA and Utility Regulator, held two well attended stakeholder engagement workshops in June and July to facilitate discussion on key issues raised in the paper.  The CfE closed on 23rd August 2024 with 50 responses from a range of key stakeholders, including energy networks, the anaerobic digestion sector, businesses, the agricultural community and academia.  Analysis of the responses is ongoing, and a report providing an overview of responses and planned next steps will be published as soon as possible.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to provide an update on the current status of Maedi Visna in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: Maedi-Visna is a notifiable disease in Northern Ireland. Where suspected the Department policy is to restrict the holding. There are currently 8 flocks restricted for Maedi Visna control reasons.  Movements onto and off the premises are restricted and are only permitted subject to the conditions of a licence.  Movements off the premises are restricted to direct slaughter in Northern Ireland and the Republic of Ireland but movements to Great Britain are permitted provided the sheep is not individually restricted.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to outline the current budget associated with the screening of Maedi Visna in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: The Department does not have a screening process in place for Maedi Visna (MV) in Northern Ireland (NI) resident flocks.  At present, the MV screening process is limited to imported sheep and goats depending on their origin.To date in 2024, the laboratory cost for post import MV sampling is expected to be approximately £4000.  Additional costs incurred by the Department will also include staff time and expenses to carry out sampling activities.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the total amount of Invest NI funding awarded in Fermanagh and South Tyrone in the last two years.
answer_text: Invest NI reports its performance on a financial year basis with the year ending 31st March 2024 being the most recent available.  Over the period 1st April 2022 to the 31st March 2024, Invest NI offered support to businesses totalling £7.31m within the Parliamentary Constituency of Fermanagh and South Tyrone. In addition to financial support Invest NI also provides wider support to all businesses through advice, assistance or guidance from its staff and through nibusinessinfo.co.uk, our online business advice portal.	For the Member’s future reference, Invest NI proactively publishes a full breakdown of financial support offered to every business over the past 5 years on the NI Open Data online portal which can be accessed from the following link: Invest NI Open Data.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, in light of the announcement of the A5 Western Transport Corridor project, to detail the assistance that can be provided to farmers for alternative replacement buildings where land has been vested.
answer_text: My Department has no specific assistance for replacement buildings for the farmers affected by the A5 Western Transport Corridor project. The project is the responsibility of the Department for Infrastructure.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, in light of the announcement of the A5 Western Transport Corridor project, to detail (i) the timeline for compensation payments; and (ii) whether payments will be made in full in one tranche or tiered.
answer_text: The payment of compensation by my department is dependent on an individual’s circumstances and personal preferences.  There is no one standard approach to this activity.Land & Property Services, who fall under the Department of Finance, have a suite of documents, intended for owners and occupiers, offering guidance on the assessment of claims for compensation arising out of compulsory acquisition for any major road scheme. They can be viewed at: Compulsory purchase and compensation guidance | Department of Finance (finance-ni.gov.uk).

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on Project Gigabit.
answer_text: My Department carried out an Open Market Review with suppliers over December 2022 and January 2023 to establish existing and planned availability of gigabit-capable broadband. This was followed by a public review between June and July 2023 to validate the results of the Open Market Review. A public review outcome report was published in February 2024.  Taking account of information provided during the National Rolling Open Market Review on operator’s plans in June as well as information gained during Pre-Procurement Market Engagement with interested suppliers, my Department expects to launch a procurement in the coming weeks. The procurement will seek a supplier to address an initial scope of approximately 11,000 premises. My Department expects to award a contract for Project Gigabit by March 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the proposed expansion of Ireland’s Hidden Heartlands through Fermanagh and South Tyrone.
answer_text: The All Island Strategic Tourism Group, established following agreement by the North South Ministerial Council, will optimise broader collaboration on opportunities that will support the sustainable growth of the tourism sector across Ireland. The group held its first meeting on 3 September 2024 to consider areas for further collaboration, including opportunities to include parts of the North into the Hidden Heartlands brand. The upcoming work led by the relevant tourism agencies will include; a facilitated industry workshop with key tourism businesses, agencies and stakeholders in the Fermanagh region; the creation of content for inclusion in promotional campaigns; and agreement on when the promotion will commence.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) whether he has any intention to update the Reservoirs Act (Northern Ireland) 2015; and (ii) for a timeframe on current developments.
answer_text: My Department is currently progressing five pieces of secondary legislation that will further commence and implement the Reservoirs Act (Northern Ireland) 2015. This legislation will cover the main provisions of the Act and I plan to introduce this shortly.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the redevelopment of Enniskillen Royal Grammar School.
answer_text: The major capital works project for Enniskillen Royal Grammar School is in the technical design and pre-tender stage (RIBA Stage 4).A planning application for the new build was submitted in March 2023, however no decision on the application has been taken at this time. Given the severe constraints on my department’s capital budget, regrettably this project will be unable to progress to construction without additional funding being secured.  However, I can assure you that I have and will continue to make the case for significant additional and sustained capital funding for my department, to permit projects like Enniskillen Royal Grammar School to proceed to construction as soon as possible.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether a final decision has been taken on the future of Lack Primary School, Enniskillen.
answer_text: On 20 September 2023, the Education Authority (EA) published Development Proposal 721 proposing that:Lack Primary School will discontinue with effect from 31 August 2024, or as soon as possible thereafter.  The statutory two-month objection period in relation to this proposal ended on 27 November 2023.A decision has not yet been taken on the proposal, however, a report has been prepared and has been passed to me to inform my decision.
